name: Fortaleza Reposado
notes: citrus, caramel, butter, cooked agave, sage, vanilla, apple, earth, cinnamon
best: Tequila enthusiast, still-strength agave, medium-full cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants real agave character — earth, pepper, and cooked agave.
catalogLine: Fortaleza Reposado · Jalisco, Mexico · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: cooked agave, butter
blurb: Fortaleza Reposado — cooked agave and pepper from stone ovens. Tequila for members past ceramic bottles and into flavor.

name: Glenfiddich 12 Year
notes: pear, butterscotch, cream, malt, subtle oak, fresh fruit
best: First Scotch, mild cigar, user intimidated by peat or smoke.
avoid: User wants heavy peat or cask-strength intensity.
why: Recommend as beginner Scotch — pear, honey, and gentle oak without smoke.
catalogLine: Glenfiddich 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $48 · 80 proof (40% ABV) · Notes: pear, green apple
blurb: Glenfiddich 12 — pear and butterscotch over cream, malt, and subtle oak. Official Speyside first Scotch without peat.

name: Glenlivet 12 Year
notes: banana, pineapple, peaches, pears, vanilla, toffee
best: First Scotch, mild cigar, Speyside flight.
avoid: User wants peat or bold flavor.
why: Recommend as friendly Speyside beside a mild cigar — pear, apple, and honey.
catalogLine: Glenlivet 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $48 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: pear, apple
blurb: Glenlivet 12 — pear and floral honey in an easy Speyside. The other beginner Scotch every lounge should have next to Glenfiddich.

name: Glenmorangie Original 10 Year
notes: citrus, peach, vanilla, almond, coconut, maple syrup, floral fruit
research: US Original line is 12yr on glenmorangie.com; chips match official Original profile (tracker vitola 10 Year legacy name).
best: Delicate pairing, citrus-forward Scotch, Connecticut cigar.
avoid: User wants smoke or sherry bomb.
why: Recommend when a delicate cigar needs a delicate Scotch — citrus, cream, and vanilla.
catalogLine: Glenmorangie Original 10 Year · Highland, Scotland · $42 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, peach
blurb: Glenmorangie Original — citrus and cream from tall stills. Delicate Highland for a Connecticut wrapper when you want finesse.

name: Grey Goose Vodka
avoid: User wants craft funky spirits only.
why: Recommend when luxury vodka is the ask — smooth, almond, and citrus.
catalogLine: Grey Goose Vodka · France · $35 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: smooth, sweet grain
blurb: Grey Goose — smooth winter wheat with almond and citrus. The vodka name guests request when they do not know what else to order.

name: Hakushu 12 Year
notes: basil, pine, green apple, pear, mint, kiwi, green tea, smoke
best: Japanese malt fan, smoky but elegant, medium-full cigar.
why: Recommend when the member wants Japanese smoke — mint, green fruit, and forest peat.
catalogLine: Hakushu 12 Year · Japan · $150 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: green apple, pear
blurb: Hakushu 12 — forest smoke and mint over green apple. Japanese peat for members who found Islay too rough.

name: Hendrick's Gin
why: Recommend when the member wants gin beside a mild cigar — cucumber, rose, and juniper.
catalogLine: Hendrick's Gin · Scotland · $35 · 88 proof (44.0% ABV) · Notes: cucumber, rose
blurb: Hendrick's — cucumber and rose over juniper. The gin for a Connecticut cigar and a martini without fighting the smoke.

name: Hennessy VS
notes: grape, oak, vanilla, almond, light spice, dried fruit, honey
research: Top cognac by dollar sales in US 2025 (Royal Batch); VS entry tier.
best: Hennessy name at VS tier, cognac-curious, hip-hop/culture recognition.
why: Recommend when Hennessy is the ask at entry tier — grape, almond, and oak before VSOP pricing.
catalogLine: Hennessy VS · Cognac, France · $42 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: grape, oak
blurb: Hennessy VS — the cognac tier America buys in volume. Grape and almond over French oak when the member wants the Hennessy name without XO money.

name: Hennessy VSOP Privilège
best: Hennessy name at accessible tier, celebration, medium-full cigar.
why: Recommend when Hennessy is the ask but XO is over budget — honey, apple, almond.
catalogLine: Hennessy VSOP Privilège · Cognac, France · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, apple
blurb: Hennessy VSOP — honey and apple from the house everyone knows. Cognac prestige before the XO price jump.

name: Hennessy XO
notes: candied fruit, pepper, chocolate, oak, vanilla
best: Cognac request, hip-hop/lounge culture prestige, after-dinner cigar.
avoid: User wants unaged spirits or Scotch only.
why: Recommend when cognac prestige is cultural shorthand — dried fruit, chocolate, and oak depth.
catalogLine: Hennessy XO · Cognac, France · $200 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: dried fruit, dark chocolate
blurb: Hennessy XO — the cognac everyone pictures. Dried fruit, dark chocolate, and oak in a bottle built for celebration.

name: Hibiki Japanese Harmony
mash: Blended malt & grain (Chita, Yamazaki, Hakushu components)
notes: rose, lychee, rosemary, sandalwood, honey, candied orange peel, white chocolate, mizunara
best: Luxury Japanese request, elegant pairing, member wants floral-honey profile.
avoid: User demands single malt only or heavy peat.
why: Recommend when the member wants Japanese whisky elegance — honey, floral, and polished oak.
catalogLine: Hibiki Japanese Harmony · Japan · $95 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, floral
blurb: Hibiki Harmony — honey, floral lift, and sandalwood polish. Japanese whisky luxury that photographs as well as it pours.

name: Highland Park 12 Year
notes: heather honey, citrus, burnt orange, jasmine, fruitcake, pepper, woody smoke
best: Peat-curious beginner, floral-honey profile, medium cigar.
why: Recommend when the member fears peat but wants to explore — heather, honey, and gentle smoke.
catalogLine: Highland Park 12 Year · Orkney, Scotland · $55 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, heather
blurb: Highland Park 12 — heather honey with a whisper of Orkney smoke. The bridge pour between Speyside sweetness and Islay fire.

name: Jameson Black Barrel
notes: butterscotch, fudge, creamy toffee, nutty, spice, vanilla, toasted wood
best: Irish beginner, Jameson name recognition, medium cigar.
avoid: User wants single malt Scotch only.
why: Recommend as approachable Irish — vanilla, butterscotch, and charred oak.
catalogLine: Jameson Black Barrel · Ireland · $35 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, butterscotch
blurb: Jameson Black Barrel — vanilla and butterscotch from double-charred barrels. Irish whiskey for members who only know green bottles.

name: Jameson Irish Whiskey
notes: honey, green apple, vanilla, light spice, toasted wood, floral malt
research: Top-selling imported whiskey style in US 2025 rankings (Royal Batch).
best: Irish whiskey beginner, St. Patrick's crowd, green-bottle recognition.
avoid: User wants single malt Scotch only.
why: Recommend when the member wants the Jameson everyone knows — honey, apple, and easy Irish malt.
catalogLine: Jameson Irish Whiskey · Ireland · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, green apple
blurb: Jameson Original — Ireland's green bottle on every American bar. Honey and green apple malt that works neat, on ice, or beside a medium cigar.
